---
title: "Setup"
subtitle: "Imports and Helper Functions"
---

## Imports

All pages in this tutorial rely on the following standard packages. These are chosen for Pyodide compatibility.

```{python}
#| label: setup
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.base import clone
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans, AgglomerativeClustering
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler

RNG = np.random.default_rng(42)
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 100)
```

## Helper functions

Five shared utilities are used throughout the tutorial pages.

```{python}
#| label: helper-functions

def evaluate_kmeans_range(X, k_values, random_state=42, n_init=20):
    rows = []
    for k in k_values:
        model = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=random_state, n_init=n_init)
        labels = model.fit_predict(X)
        rows.append({
            "k": k,
            "inertia": model.inertia_,
            "silhouette": silhouette_score(X, labels)
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def add_segment_order(summary_df, sort_col, ascending=True):
    ordered = summary_df.sort_values(sort_col, ascending=ascending).copy()
    ordered.insert(0, "segment_rank", range(1, len(ordered) + 1))
    return ordered


def plot_metric_curve(df_metrics, x_col="k", y_cols=("inertia", "silhouette"), title_prefix=""):
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

    axes[0].plot(df_metrics[x_col], df_metrics[y_cols[0]], marker="o")
    axes[0].set_title(f"{title_prefix}Inertia by k")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("k")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("Inertia")

    axes[1].plot(df_metrics[x_col], df_metrics[y_cols[1]], marker="o")
    axes[1].set_title(f"{title_prefix}Silhouette by k")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("k")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Silhouette")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


def plot_pca_segments(X, labels, title="PCA view of segments"):
    if hasattr(X, "toarray"):
        X = X.toarray()
    pca = PCA(n_components=2, random_state=42)
    coords = pca.fit_transform(X)

    plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
    scatter = plt.scatter(coords[:, 0], coords[:, 1], c=labels)
    plt.title(title)
    plt.xlabel("PC1")
    plt.ylabel("PC2")
    plt.show()


def top_categories_by_segment(df, segment_col, cat_cols):
    pieces = []
    for col in cat_cols:
        mode_vals = (
            df.groupby(segment_col)[col]
            .agg(lambda s: s.mode().iloc[0] if not s.mode().empty else np.nan)
            .rename(col)
        )
        pieces.append(mode_vals)
    return pd.concat(pieces, axis=1)
```

**`evaluate_kmeans_range(X, k_values)`** — fits K-means for each value of k and returns a DataFrame of inertia and silhouette scores.

**`add_segment_order(summary_df, sort_col)`** — inserts a `segment_rank` column sorted by a chosen column, useful for profiling tables.

**`plot_metric_curve(df_metrics)`** — plots inertia and silhouette side by side to help choose k.

**`plot_pca_segments(X, labels)`** — projects the feature matrix to two principal components and plots colored by cluster label.

**`top_categories_by_segment(df, segment_col, cat_cols)`** — returns the modal category value for each categorical column by segment.

## Session info

```{python}
#| label: session-info
import sys
import sklearn

print("Python:", sys.version.split()[0])
print("pandas:", pd.__version__)
print("numpy:", np.__version__)
print("scikit-learn:", sklearn.__version__)
```
